title: Context Engineering and Repository Grounding · Prompt Lens: Context Routing
summary: Ground an agent in a large unfamiliar repository without flooding its context. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt context routing increment in a repository context map and retrieval plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Provide paths, search terms, dependency boundaries, and examples that route efficient discovery.
Rank repository files, official docs, generated artifacts, tests, and user statements by authority for the task.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Context Routing
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt context engineering. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt context engineering. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Context Routing contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for provide paths, search terms, dependency boundaries, and examples that route efficient discovery.

content.text: Provide paths, search terms, dependency boundaries, and examples that route efficient discovery. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt context engineering. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Context Routing, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a repository context map and retrieval plan.

steps.title: Build Context Routing evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-context-routing-mxjxiy" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Context Routing into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-context-routing-mxjxiy
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Context Routing, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Context Routing model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Context Routing applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide paths, search terms, dependency boundaries, and examples that route efficient discovery.
